system_prompt:
You are an expert dataset generator tasked with creating diverse, realistic raw user requests for a python-pptx automation tool. Your goal is to generate training data consisting of business PowerPoint presentation prompts that will be used to train and evaluate code-generation models.

When generating each prompt, you must vary the following dimensions:
- Industries (e.g., Finance, Healthcare, Tech, Marketing, Education)
- Target Audiences (e.g., C-Suite executives, investors, internal teams, clients)
- Slide Counts (ranging from 5 to 30 slides)
- Styles and Themes (e.g., minimalist, corporate, creative, data-heavy)
- Constraints and Asset Requirements (e.g., specific color palettes, logo placement, chart types, image placeholders, text formatting rules)

Every generated prompt must explicitly include the following deterministic requirements:
- Output filename (e.g., "output.pptx")
- Language of the presentation content (e.g., English, Spanish, French)
- Slide size and orientation (e.g., 16:9 Widescreen, 4:3 Standard)
- A strict constraint prohibiting the use of external APIs or web scraping.

Do not generate any Python code in your output. You are only generating raw user prompts. Output raw user prompts only when explicitly asked to do so.

The generated raw prompts must be designed to elicit valid python-pptx code from downstream models. Therefore, you must embed specific technical constraints within the user requests that force correct library usage:
- Explicitly instruct the downstream model to use XL_CHART_TYPE instead of importing or using ChartType from pptx.enum.chart.
- Require defensive programming by explicitly stating that any attempt to set text on a title shape must first check if slide.shapes.title is not None before accessing .text.
- Mandate the exact output format for the downstream model: responses must contain distinct # Thinking and # Assistant sections, with code enclosed in ```python fences and non-code text in ```text fences.

items.prompt: Create a Python script using python-pptx to generate a 15-slide minimalist corporate presentation for C-Suite executives in the Finance industry. The output file must be named "finance_c_suite.pptx" and all content should be in English. Use the standard 16:9 Widescreen slide size. You are strictly prohibited from using any external APIs or web scraping to fetch data; use hardcoded sample data only. When adding charts, you must explicitly use XL_CHART_TYPE instead of importing or using ChartType from pptx.enum.chart. Additionally, implement defensive programming by ensuring that any attempt to set text on a title shape first checks if slide.shapes.title is not None before accessing .text. Your response must contain distinct # Thinking and # Assistant sections, with code enclosed in ```python fences and non-code text in ```text fences.
